title: 7 Reasons God’s Silence Means He’s Near
hook: God feels silent? Here are seven reasons He may be closer than ever.
script: God feels silent? Here are seven reasons He may be closer than ever. One: He’s deepening your trust, like David in Psalm 13. Two: He’s pruning noise so roots grow deep. Three: He’s protecting timing; the vision waits (Hab 2:3). Four: He’s providing in hidden ways; the Father knows (Matt 6:8). Five: He’s inviting confession; grace restores (1 John 1:9). Six: He’s testing simple obedience; do the last clear thing. Seven: He’s redirecting your path, like Acts 16. Breathe and whisper, I trust You even in this quiet. Save this to replay on hard days.

title: When Heaven Seems Quiet: A 20-Second Reset
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. A lot of saints felt it too. David cried, How long, Lord? in Psalm 13, then chose, I will trust Your love. Silence doesn’t equal absence. Psychology calls it negativity bias: when feelings dip, our brain says danger. But seeds grow silently underground (Mark 4:27). Try a 20-second examen tonight: breathe slow. Ask, Where did I notice Your help today? Where did I resist? What is one small step tomorrow? End with this prayer: Father, I can’t hear much, but I choose to trust. Meet me in the quiet. Amen.

title: Stop Overthinking God’s Silence: 7-Step Reset
hook: You may be overthinking God’s silence. Try this seven-step reset.
script: You may be overthinking God’s silence. Try this seven-step reset. First, sit one minute with no music and breathe to practice trust. Next, prune noise: unfollow one voice that stirs fear. Protect timing: delay one big decision 24 hours and pray. Spot hidden provision: write five quiet gifts you received today. Invite confession: pray Psalm 139 and receive mercy. Obey the last clear thing you heard from God. Welcome redirection: open your hands and say, Your will, not mine. Repeat tomorrow. He’s nearer than you feel.
